topic: truck stop food ranking — worst biscuits and gravy in Georgia

turns.content: man let me tell you about the worst biscuits and gravy i have ever put in my mouth in my entire life and i have eaten at every truck stop in the southeast so this is not a ranking i give lightly
Haha okay I'm ready for this. Where was this culinary disaster? And how bad are we talking — just mediocre or actually offensive?

offensive is the right word there is this place off i-16 in georgia between macon and savannah i dont even want to say the name but you know those truck stops that have a little diner attached with the fluorescent lights that make everything look gray yeah well i ordered biscuits and gravy at like 1am because i was starving and what they brought me looked like somebody poured wallpaper paste over two hockey pucks

Wallpaper paste over hockey pucks is an incredible description. I feel like I can see it. Was the gravy the main problem or were the biscuits equally tragic? Because a bad biscuit can sometimes be saved by good gravy, but bad gravy ruins everything.

both were bad but the gravy was the real crime it had no flavor like literally zero seasoning no pepper no sausage chunks just this white paste that tasted like flour and water and maybe a ghost of a pork product that walked through the kitchen once and the biscuits were the frozen kind you know the ones that come in a bag and they just microwave them so they are dense and rubbery on the inside and weirdly crispy on the outside

A ghost of a pork product that walked through the kitchen once — I'm dying. That's the saddest gravy description I've ever heard. Frozen microwaved biscuits are a crime against the south honestly. Georgia of all places should know better. Did you eat it anyway or did you walk away?

of course i ate it man when youre driving at 1am and thats the only thing open you eat what they give you thats the truck driver life you dont get to be picky i put hot sauce on it like enough hot sauce to kill whatever was in there and just powered through it but i was thinking about it the whole rest of the drive like how do you mess up biscuits and gravy thats like the simplest dish in america

The hot sauce rescue mission — every trucker's secret weapon. You're right though, biscuits and gravy is supposed to be foolproof. It's literally fat, flour, milk, sausage, salt, and pepper over a biscuit. The bar is on the floor and they still tripped over it.

So where do the best biscuits and gravy live in your experience? You must have a gold standard after all those miles.

okay so the best ones i ever had were at this little place outside of chattanooga off i-24 its not even a truck stop its like a gas station with a kitchen in the back run by this older lady who makes everything from scratch the biscuits are so tall and fluffy and the gravy has real chunks of sausage with black pepper and you can taste the sage and it comes in a big styrofoam bowl for like $6 i was listening to this podcast about southern food traditions and they were saying the best food is always at the places that look the worst from the outside and man that is 100 percent true

That's the universal law of roadside food — the sketchier the building looks, the better the food. A gas station kitchen run by one lady who makes everything from scratch? That's the pinnacle. $6 for a big styrofoam bowl of the real thing is a steal too.

You should start a trucker food blog honestly. You've got the miles and the taste buds for it. Rate every truck stop diner on the biscuit and gravy scale.

man i would but who is going to read a truck drivers food blog you know actually maybe a lot of people would because truck drivers are always asking each other where to eat thats like the number one conversation at any truck stop parking lot hey where did you eat last night was it any good and everybody has opinions but nobody writes it down i should make a list at least for myself so i remember where not to stop you know the georgia place is on the permanent no list forever

There are actually a few trucker food apps out there but they're pretty basic — your firsthand reviews would be way more useful. Even just a notes app list of "yes" and "never again" spots organized by highway would be gold for other drivers.

And hey, that Georgia place might have saved someone's life — if your review keeps even one trucker from eating wallpaper paste at 1am, you've done a public service. The I-16 Hockey Puck Biscuit Warning should be posted at the on-ramp.
